item.title: 从油价飙升到股市震荡：中东冲突如何牵动全球市场
item.description: 中东地区冲突升级令全球市场周一动荡不安，持久战前景推高能源价格，重创旅游类股票及区域性资产。【原文：An escalating conflict in the Middle East rattled global markets on Monday, as the prospect of a long-drawn fight drove energy prices higher and hammered travel stocks and ​regional assets.】

item.title: 美国财政部将终止使用Anthropic所有产品，Bessent如是说
item.description: 美国财政部部长斯科特·贝森特周一在社交媒体发文称，该部门将终止使用Anthropic旗下所有产品，包括其Claude平台。【原文：The U.S ​Treasury Department ‌is terminating ​all ​use of Anthropic ⁠products, ​including ​use of its Claude ​platform, ​Secretary Scott Bessent ‌said ⁠a social media ​post ​on ⁠Monday.】

item.title: 消息人士称，即便签署协议后，美国仍努力降低刚果"战区矿产"的风险
item.description: 外交官和行业人士表示，尽管美国在推动刚果战略矿产脱离中国掌控方面取得进展，但冲突、矿权争议和合规要求仍在拖慢华盛顿进军该地区的步伐——这个地区正由其竞争对手主导。【原文：The U.S. has made progress in its push to prise Congo's strategic minerals from China's orbit, but conflict, contested licences and compliance demands are still slowing Washington's advance into a region its rival dominates, diplomats and ​industry officials said.】

item.title: 纳斯达克寻求美国证券交易委员会批准在主要股指上推出预测市场期权
item.description: 纳斯达克已向美国证券交易委员会提交申请，寻求批准在主要股指上推出预测市场期权，根据监管文件显示。【原文：Nasdaq has sought approval from the U.S. Securities and ​Exchange Commission to roll ‌out prediction markets options on a major ​stock index, ​according to a regulatory filing.】

item.title: T-Mobile就虚假广告承诺指控反诉Verizon Wireless
item.description: T-Mobile对Verizon Wireless提起反诉，指控其最大竞争对手采用"诱饵式营销"广告诱导消费者更换手机运营商。【原文：T-Mobile countersued Verizon Wireless , accusing its largest rival of engaging in "bait-and-switch" advertising to induce consumers to ​switch cellphone carriers.】

item.title: 美国商品期货交易委员会任命前检察官兼加密货币律师担任首席执法官
item.description: 美国商品期货交易委员会周一宣布，前检察官戴维·米勒将出任该机构执法部门最高官员。随着该机构准备加强对加密货币和预测市场的监管力度，这一人事任命具有重要意义。【原文：The U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission said on Monday that former ​prosecutor David Miller will become its top enforcement official, as ‌the agency prepares to take on greater oversight of cryptocurrency and prediction markets.】

item.title: 美国2月制造业稳步增长，投入价格飙升
item.description: 美国2月份制造业活动稳步扩张，但工厂为投入品支付的价格指数升至近三年半高位，凸显出在进口关税背景下通胀存在上行风险——这甚至早于美国主导的对伊朗军事行动推高油价之前。【原文：U.S. manufacturing activity expanded steadily in February, but a measure of prices paid by factories for inputs rose to the highest level in nearly 3-1/2 years, highlighting upside risks to inflation amid import tariffs even before ​a U.S.-led attack on Iran sent oil prices higher.】

item.title: 美国最高法院拒绝审理人工智能生成内容的版权纠纷
item.description: 美国最高法院周一拒绝受理关于人工智能创作的艺术作品能否在美国法律下获得版权保护的案件，驳回了密苏里州一名计算机科学家的诉讼请求。该科学家此前因其人工智能系统创作的视觉艺术作品被拒绝授予版权而提起诉讼。【原文：The U.S. Supreme Court declined on Monday to take up the ​issue of whether art generated by artificial intelligence can be copyrighted under U.S. law, turning ‌away a case involving a computer scientist from Missouri who was denied a copyright for a piece of visual art made by his AI system.】

item.title: 中国Minimax公司报告强劲营收增长，规划更宏大的AI蓝图
item.description: 中国人工智能初创企业MiniMax周一宣布，计划在2025年实现强劲营收增长后，加速扩张并拓宽产品线，致力于成为全球人工智能平台公司。【原文：Chinese artificial intelligence startup MiniMax aims to become a global AI platform company, it said on Monday, setting out plans for faster expansion and a broader product lineup after posting strong 2025 revenue growth.】

item.title: 派拉蒙债务将达790亿美元，收购华纳兄弟后无意出售有线资产
item.description: 派拉蒙周一表示，华纳兄弟与派拉蒙的合并将形成一个净债务约790亿美元的实体，同时该公司排除了剥离或分拆有线电视资产的任何计划。【原文：The Warner Bros-Paramount Skydance merger will create a combined entity that would have a net debt of about $79 billion, Paramount said on Monday, as it ruled out any plan to divest or ​spinoff the cable assets.】

item.title: 苹果推出iPhone 17e，起售价599美元
item.description: 苹果公司周一推出iPhone 17e，这款更实惠的智能手机起售价为599美元，配备256GB存储空间，旨在捍卫其在全球竞争激烈的手机市场中的份额。【原文：Apple on Monday launched iPhone 17e, its more affordable smartphone model that starts at a price of $599 with 256 gigabytes of storage, as it looks to defend its share in a fiercely competitive global handset market.】

item.title: 巴西航空工业公司对美国恢复零关税表示欢迎，并预测行业将实现增长
item.description: 巴西飞机制造商埃姆布拉尔公司首席执行官弗朗西斯科·戈麦斯·内托周一表示，随着美国恢复零关税政策，该公司预计将实现"显著增长"。【原文：Brazilian planemaker Embraer expects "substantial growth" supported by the United States' return to a zero tariff policy, CEO Francisco Gomes Neto said on Monday.】

item.title: Hims的扩张可能来不及赶上风险较高的GLP-1业务
item.description: 远程医疗公司Hims & Hers Health以减肥药物业务闻名，该公司承诺将实现业务多元化，摆脱对美国复合GLP-1销售的依赖。但对于日益失去耐心的投资者而言，这一转变可能来得太迟。【原文：Telehealth company Hims & Hers Health , known for its weight-loss drug business, is promising it can diversify away from its reliance on compounded GLP-1 sales in the U.S., but that may not happen soon enough for investors who are growing impatient.】

item.title: 汤森路透任命戴尔前高管比绍平为首席财务官
item.description: 汤森路透周一宣布，任命戴尔公司前高管小加里·E·比绍平为公司首席财务官，将于5月8日正式就任。他将接替迈克·伊斯伍德的职位，后者将按计划退休。【原文：Thomson Reuters on Monday named former Dell executive Gary E. Bischoping Jr. as its chief financial officer beginning May 8, succeeding Mike Eastwood, who will retire as part of a planned transition.】

item.title: 英伟达将向光子产品制造商Lumentum和Coherent投资40亿美元
item.description: 英伟达宣布将分别向光子产品制造商Lumentum和Coherent投资20亿美元，以支持这两家公司在美国的研发和制造业务。【原文：Nvidia will invest $2 billion each in photonic product makers Lumentum and Coherent to support the companies' research and development and manufacturing operations in the U.S., the companies said on Monday.】

item.title: 德国电信与星链合作扩大移动网络覆盖范围
item.description: 德国电信周一宣布，将与埃隆·马斯克的星链公司合作，在欧洲移动信号覆盖薄弱的地区提供基于卫星的直接连接服务。【原文：Deutsche Telekom said on Monday it was partnering with Elon Musk's Starlink to offer satellite-based direct connectivity in areas with poor mobile reception in Europe.】

item.title: 紧张局势升级，科技巨头在中东的人工智能投资成为焦点
item.description: 美国与以色列针对伊朗的空袭行动不断升级，给该地区带来了新的不确定性。该地区正致力于打造全球科技与人工智能中心，已吸引数十亿美元的投资承诺。【原文：The expanding U.S.-Israeli air war against Iran has sparked fresh uncertainty in a region that has attracted billions of dollars of investment commitments as it positioned itself as a global technology and artificial intelligence hub.】

item.title: 伊朗冲突扰乱全球航运，油轮受困受损
item.description: 随着伊朗冲突扩大导致航运中断，保险公司正取消对波斯湾船舶的战争风险保险。目前已有至少四艘油轮受损，两名海员遇难，150艘船只在霍尔木兹海峡附近受困。【原文：Insurance companies are cancelling war risk coverage for vessels in the Gulf as the widening Iran conflict disrupted shipping, leaving at least four tankers damaged, two seafarers killed and 150 ships stranded around the Strait of Hormuz.】

item.title: 贝莱德旗下全球基础设施部门与EQT集团以334亿美元收购AES公司
item.description: 由贝莱德旗下全球基础设施合伙公司与私募股权公司EQT AB牵头的财团周一达成协议，将以334亿美元（含债务）收购AES公司。【原文：A consortium led by BlackRock-owned Global Infrastructure Partners and equity firm EQT AB on Monday agreed to acquire AES Corp for $33.4 billion, including debt.】

item.title: 土耳其正义与发展党提交法案，拟对加密货币收入征税并对服务提供商加征税费
item.description: 土耳其执政党正义与发展党周一向议会提交了一项法案草案，该法案拟对加密货币收入征税，并对加密资产服务提供商征收交易税。【原文：Turkey's ruling AK Party on Monday submitted to parliament a draft law that would introduce a tax on cryptocurrency income and a transaction levy on crypto asset service providers.】

item.title: 晨间竞标：中东漩涡
item.description: 当今美国及全球市场中真正重要的因素【原文：What matters in U.S. and global markets today】

item.title: 挪威邮轮公司年度利润预测低于预期
item.description: 挪威邮轮控股公司周一预测年度利润低于华尔街预期，原因是高昂成本抵消了对其高价航程的强劲需求。【原文：Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings forecast annual profit below Wall Street expectations on Monday as steep costs offset robust demand for its higher-priced voyages.】

item.title: 亚马逊将在西班牙追加投资210亿美元用于数据中心和人工智能建设
item.description: 亚马逊周一宣布，将额外向西班牙投资180亿欧元（约合210亿美元），用于扩建数据中心并推动人工智能创新，使该公司在西班牙的总投资额达到337亿欧元。【原文：Amazon said on Monday it would invest an additional 18 billion euros ($21 billion) in Spain to expand its data centres and boost AI innovation, bringing its total investment in the country to 33.7 billion euros.】

item.title: 多伦多证券交易所期货下跌，因中东紧张局势升级
item.description: 加拿大主要股指期货周一下跌，反映出全球股市的跌势，因中东地区紧张局势加剧，引发市场担忧冲突可能持续数周。【原文：Futures for Canada's main stock index fell on Monday, mirroring declines in global equities as mounting tensions in the Middle East stoked fears of a conflict that could drag on for weeks.】

item.title: 托克集团与Venture Global签署五年期美国液化天然气供应协议
item.description: Venture Global与商品贸易商托克集团周一宣布，双方已达成协议，自2026年起，Venture Global将向托克集团供应约50万吨/年的美国液化天然气，为期五年。【原文：Venture Global has agreed to sell about 0.5 million tonnes per annum (MTPA) of U.S. liquefied natural gas to commodity trader Trafigura for five years, starting in 2026, the companies said on Monday.】

item.title: 独家：金融科技公司Pine Labs首席执行官称将推出印度境外稳定币支付服务
item.description: 金融科技公司Pine Labs首席执行官向路透社透露，该公司将于4月底前在中东、非洲及东南亚九国推出稳定币支持的预付卡，这标志着印度支付巨头首次尝试进军这个快速增长的市场。【原文：Pine Labs will launch a stablecoin-backed prepaid card across nine countries in the Middle East, Africa and Southeast Asia by the end of April, the fintech firm's chief executive told Reuters, marking the first attempt by an Indian payments major to tap the fast‑growing market.】

item.title: 特朗普领导的美国证券交易委员会赋予企业更多控制投资者的权力。诉讼迫使他们让步。
item.description: 活动人士表示，美国政府推出一项新政策，赋予企业更多权力决定股东能在年度会议上对哪些提案进行投票，这正引发监管不确定性并导致诉讼案件增加。【原文：A new federal approach giving U.S. companies more say over which proposals shareholders can vote on at their annual meetings is creating regulatory uncertainty and leading to litigation, activists said.】

item.title: 独家：ASML规划超越EUV的AI芯片制造设备未来蓝图
item.description: ASML控股公司高管向路透社透露，该公司计划将芯片制造设备产品线扩展至多个新领域，以抢占快速增长的人工智能芯片市场份额。【原文：ASML Holding has ambitious plans to expand its line of chipmaking equipment into several new products to capture more of the rapidly growing market for artificial intelligence chips, a senior executive told Reuters.】

item.title: 南弓计划重启基石XL部分段落需获特朗普批准，美国输油管道关联
item.description: 由加拿大南方弓公司主导的重启部分已取消的基石XL输油管道的提案，若获得美国总统特朗普批准并增建通往美国炼油中心的连接管道，可使加拿大对美原油出口量增加12%以上。【原文：A proposal led by Canadian company South Bow to revive parts of the cancelled Keystone XL oil pipeline could increase Canada's crude exports to the U.S. by more than 12%, if it gets a green light from U.S. President Donald Trump and additional links to U.S. refining hubs are built.】

item.title: 美联储加速调整以应对人工智能对就业和通胀的机遇与风险
item.description: 美国联邦储备委员会官员们普遍认为人工智能将引发经济剧变，但如今他们正努力理解未来变革的速度与程度。关于人工智能对劳动力市场和物价的潜在影响，官员们之间出现了分歧。【原文：U.S. Federal Reserve officials who have largely accepted that artificial intelligence will lead to dramatic shifts in the economy are now struggling to understand the pace and extent of what's to come, with a divide emerging over its potential to impact the labor market and prices.】
